Summary: This study developed a deep learning model to distinguish pseudoprogression (PsP) from true progression (TP) in glioblastoma (GBM) patients using routine follow-up MRI sequences (T2 and CET1). The model achieved a mean accuracy of 76.4%, a mean AUROCC of 0.7560, a mean sensitivity of 88.72%, and a mean specificity of 62.05% using five-fold cross-validation.

Condition1.reason: The study uses HD-GLIO-AUTO to segment the MRI exams resulting in enhancing lesion volumes.

Condition2.reason: The study mentions that they manually checked all the extracted masks and made corrections when necessary, indicating that the segmentation was not fully automated.

Condition3.reason: The study uses a deep learning model (3D-DenseNet-121) to extract features directly from the images, rather than relying on hand-crafted feature extraction.

Condition4.reason: The study does not explicitly mention the use of tabular data. The deep learning model takes image data as input and produces a classification result.

Condition5.reason: The study uses a 3D-DenseNet-121 model, which is a deep learning approach, to directly process the image data and produce a classification model.
